name: rx-selection

metadata.name: rx-selection
metadata.summary: Project used to apply selection to ntuples used for RX measurements filtered and skimmed by post_ap project

documentation:
# $R_X$ selection

This project is meant to apply an offline selection to ntuples produced by
[post_ap](https://github.com/acampove/post_ap/tree/main/src/post_ap_scripts)
and downloaded with
[rx_data](https://github.com/acampove/rx_data).
the selection is done with jobs sent to an HTCondor cluster.

## Usage

For local tests one can use `apply_selection` as in:

```bash
apply_selection -d /home/acampove/Data/rx_samples/v1/post_ap -s data_24_magdown_24c2 -r q2 bdt -q central -t Hlt2RD_B0ToKpPimMuMu -p RK -i 0 -n 900
```

where:

```bash
usage: apply_selection [-h] -i IPART -n NPART -d IPATH -s SAMPLE -p PROJECT -q Q2BIN -t HLT2 [-c CUTVER] [-r REMOVE [REMOVE ...]]

Script used to apply selection and produce samples for Run 3

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -i IPART, --ipart IPART
                        Part to process
  -n NPART, --npart NPART
                        Total number of parts
  -d IPATH, --ipath IPATH
                        Path to directory with subdirectories with samples
  -s SAMPLE, --sample SAMPLE
                        Name of sample to process, e.g. data_24_magdown_24c2
  -p PROJECT, --project PROJECT
                        Name of project, e.g RK, RKstr
  -q Q2BIN, --q2bin Q2BIN
                        q2 bin, e.g. central
  -t HLT2, --hlt2 HLT2  Name of HLT2 trigger, e.g. Hlt2RD_B0ToKpPimMuMu
  -c CUTVER, --cutver CUTVER
                        Version of selection, by default, latest
  -r REMOVE [REMOVE ...], --remove REMOVE [REMOVE ...]
                        List of cuts to remove from the full selection
```

If none is skipped, will return original cut

Takes dictionary with selection and overrides with with entries in self._d_redefine_cuts
Returns redefined dictionary

documentation:
Takes cutflow and nreco to calculate the reco efficiency and add it to the cutflow
Returns updated cutflow

methods.name: rx_selection.ds_getter.ds_getter._redefine_cut
documentation: Takes cut, checks if it is meant to be redefined, returns updated value

Class used to provide dataframe after a given selection

methods.name: rx_selection.differential_efficiency.defficiency._get_xaxis
documentation:
Takes list of bounds [(min, max)...] 
returns list of bin centers and assymetric errors
Removes under and over flows and keeps track of what was kept in l_index

classes.name: rx_selection.cache_data.CacheData

methods.name: rx_selection.cache_data.CacheData._cache_path
documentation:
Picks name of directory where samples will go
Checks if ROOT file is already made
Returns path and flag, signaling that the file exists or not

methods.name: rx_selection.cache_data.CacheData.save
documentation: Will apply selection and save ROOT file

Class used to apply selection to input datasets and save selected files
It's mostly an interface to ds_getter

modules.name: rx_selection.version_management
documentation: Module containing functions used to find latest, next version, etc of a path.

functions.name: rx_selection.version_management._get_numeric_version
documentation:
Takes string with numbers at the end (padded or not)
Returns integer version of numbers

functions.name: rx_selection.version_management.get_last_version
documentation:
Returns path or just version associated to latest version found in given path

Parameters
---------------------
dir_path (str) : Path to directory where versioned subdirectories exist
version_only (bool): Returns only vxxxx if True, otherwise, full path to directory
main_only (bool): Returns vX where X is a number. Otherwise it will return vx.y in case version has subversion
extension (str) : Extension of text files for which the latest version will be found, e.g. yaml

Return
---------------------
String with name of latest version, e.g. v1

modules.name: rx_selection.efficiency
documentation: Module holding efficiency class and helper functions

classes.name: rx_selection.efficiency.InconsistentEff
documentation: When multiplying efficiencies, numerator of first one has to be equal to denominator of second one

classes.name: rx_selection.efficiency.DifferentLabel
documentation: When adding two efficiencies, labels (symbolizing sample and selection) need to be the same

classes.name: rx_selection.efficiency.ZeroYields
documentation: Numerator and denominator cannot be zero in an efficiency

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.__init__
documentation:
arg_pas: int, tuple, ndarray
arg_fal: int, tuple, ndarray
arg_tot: int, tuple, ndarray

tuple = (yield, frequency)

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.val
documentation: Returns tuple (efficiency, error_up, error_down)

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.copy
documentation: Function that optionally (to override) takes a label and cut and makes a copy of current efficiency

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.show
documentation: Prints current efficiency object, yields and efficiencies with errors

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.__add__
documentation:
Takes efficiency object and returns efficiency object
resulting from combining corresponding sample, with current
object's sample

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency._scale_sample
documentation:
Will modify the passed and failed yields for a sample that is `other` times
larger. Central value of efficiency should stay the same, errors are scaled too.

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.__mul__
documentation: Returns product of efficiency objects, current object and argument object

methods.name: rx_selection.efficiency.efficiency.__truediv__
documentation: Takes numerical value of efficiency, returns ratio of this efficiency by other efficiency

documentation: Class meant to represent efficiencies

modules.name: rx_selection.selection
documentation: Module containing the selection function, which returns a dictionary of cuts

functions.name: rx_selection.selection.selection
documentation:
Picks up sample name, trigger, etc, returns dictionary with selection

analysis : EE or MM
project  : RK or RKst
q2bin    : low, central, jpsi, psi2S or high
event_type : Event type for MC, "data" for data

documentation: Returns numerical value of total efficiency

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow.efficiency
documentation: Returns efficiency object, product of all efficiencies

documentation: Will save cutflow to JSON

classes.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager._check_nominal
documentation: Check if dictionary contains nominal efficiency

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager._pad_eff_int
documentation: Takes {sys:eff}, pads with nominal missing sistematics

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager._pad_all
documentation: Will pad with nominal (cut, syst) locations for systematics that do not make sense for given cut.

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager._get_cf_int
documentation: Takes sys string and {cut : {sys : eff...}...} and for given systematic returns cutflow object

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager._get_cf_dif
documentation:
Takes sys, var strings and {cut : {sys[,var] : [d]eff...}...},
i.e. inner dict (with sys -> eff) or ndict (with sys, var -> deff)

Returns cutflow for given sys, var combination.

methods.name: rx_selection.cutflow.cutflow_manager.get_cf
documentation:
Returns either {sys : cutflow} dict or {sys, var : cutflow} ndict

Latter is returned if one of the efficiencies is differential
Class used to build cutflow objects. It takes care of switching between efficiencies, depending on the systematics
